varieties.name: Belleville 🏆
varieties.origin: France, domesticated in 1730s
varieties.traits: Large, pale green leaves, arrow-shaped, grows 45-60cm tall
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, continuous harvest throughout growing season
varieties.availability: Available from The Seed Collection, Diggers Club, and specialist seed suppliers throughout Queensland

varieties.name: Profusion
varieties.origin: USA, introduced 1993 (patented variety)
varieties.traits: Non-flowering variety, compact growth 20-25cm, round dark green succulent leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent all-season production, no seed production means continuous leaf growth
varieties.availability: Available as plants (not seeds) from specialty herb nurseries; Poyntzfield Herb Nursery ships to Australia

varieties.name: Blonde de Lyon 🏆
varieties.origin: Lyon, France, traditional French cultivar
varieties.traits: Very large, yellow-green leaves, less prone to flowering
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy producer, slower to bolt than other varieties
varieties.availability: Available from specialty European seed importers, some Australian heirloom seed companies

varieties.name: Large Leaf Sorrel 🏆
varieties.origin: Europe, traditional cultivar
varieties.traits: Extra-large leaves up to 20cm, deep green, spinach-like appearance
varieties.yield_notes: High-yielding, produces masses of large leaves
varieties.availability: Seed Needs LLC, Bucktown Seed Company, Thresh Seed Co., widely available in Australia

varieties.name: Common Garden Sorrel 🏆
varieties.origin: Native to Europe, cultivated for centuries
varieties.traits: Arrow-shaped leaves, grows 45-90cm tall, green stems
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable producer, prolific in suitable conditions
varieties.availability: Widely available: The Seed Collection Australia, Yates, Australian seed companies

varieties.name: Buckler Leaf Sorrel 🏆
varieties.origin: France and Southern Europe, traditional variety
varieties.traits: Shield-shaped (buckler) grey-green leaves, low-growing 15-45cm, mat-forming habit
varieties.yield_notes: Productive throughout the season, continuous harvest
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery, Mudbrick Herb Cottage, specialty herb suppliers in Queensland

varieties.name: Silver Shield Sorrel 🏆
varieties.origin: Cultivar selection of Rumex scutatus
varieties.traits: Silver-overlaid leaves, compact tidy habit, ornamental
varieties.yield_notes: Good production, decorative addition to gardens
varieties.availability: Limited availability; specialty herb collectors; Farmacie Isolde (international shipping)

varieties.name: Red-Veined Sorrel 🏆
varieties.origin: Europe, heirloom cultivar
varieties.traits: Green leaves with striking red/purple veins and stems, grows 60cm x 40cm, highly ornamental
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate production, valued more for ornamental appeal
varieties.availability: Succeed Heirlooms Australia, Mudbrick Herb Cottage, specialty nurseries in humid subtropical climates/Queensland

varieties.name: Bloody Dock 🏆
varieties.origin: Common name for Rumex sanguineus varieties, European
varieties.traits: Deep red veining throughout leaves, dramatic coloring
varieties.yield_notes: Good leaf production, especially in summer
varieties.availability: Various Australian nurseries under red-veined sorrel name

varieties.name: Common Sheep Sorrel 🏆
varieties.origin: Native to Eurasia, naturalized worldwide
varieties.traits: Small arrow-shaped leaves, red-tinted stems, creeping rhizomes, grows 20-50cm
varieties.availability: Often found growing wild; available as weed in many humid subtropical climates gardens; can be harvested from naturalized populations

varieties.name: Apache Red Sheep Sorrel
varieties.origin: Selected variety of Rumex acetosella
varieties.traits: Redder coloration than common type, small leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Good microgreen producer, used for garnishes
varieties.availability: True Leaf Market (US), specialty microgreen suppliers

varieties.name: Queensland Jelly Plant 🏆
varieties.origin: Introduced to Australia from Sri Lanka, naturalized in Queensland since 1800s
varieties.traits: Tropical shrub 1.5-2.5m tall, red stems, pale yellow flowers with red centers, fleshy red calyces
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific producer in warm climates, 6 months to fruit from seed
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery, The Seed Vine, Australian Seed, Mudbrick Herb Cottage, Ole Lantana Seeds - widely available throughout Queensland

varieties.name: Rosella Standard 🏆
varieties.origin: Traditional Australian rosella variety, Queensland heritage
varieties.traits: Vigorous upright growth, red-tinged stems, grows 2-3m in humid subtropical climates conditions
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy cropping in Humid subtropical subtropical climate

varieties.name: Black Rosella
varieties.origin: Selection with darker coloration
varieties.traits: Deep magenta to purple-black calyces, darker stems
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent producer, larger calyces than standard
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery (specific cultivar mentioned by customers)

varieties.name: Super Rosella
varieties.origin: Improved selection for size
varieties.traits: Extra-large plants and calyces, vigorous growth
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yields in suitable climate
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery (specific cultivar mentioned by customers)

varieties.name: Roselle Thai Red
varieties.origin: Thai selection, Southeast Asian variety
varieties.traits: Burgundy-red calyces, productive bushy growth
varieties.availability: Specialty Asian seed suppliers, international seed companies

varieties.name: Roselle Cocktail Red
varieties.origin: Ornamental and culinary selection
varieties.traits: Decorative red stalks, attractive flowers and calyces
varieties.availability: Australian Seed supplier

varieties.name: Early Rosella
varieties.origin: Selection for earlier maturity
varieties.traits: Faster growing, earlier fruiting (5 months vs 6 months)
varieties.yield_notes: Good production with shorter growing season
varieties.availability: Ole Lantana Seeds

varieties.name: Jamaican Sorrel 🏆
varieties.origin: Caribbean traditional variety, same species as rosella
varieties.traits: Traditional Caribbean variety, red stems and calyces
varieties.availability: Specialty Caribbean seed suppliers, international seed exchanges

varieties.name: Florida Cranberry 🏆
varieties.origin: Florida traditional variety, popular 1890s-1950s
varieties.traits: Historical US variety, grown commercially in Florida
varieties.availability: US heirloom seed suppliers, international orders

varieties.name: Creeping Wood Sorrel
varieties.origin: Native to Europe, naturalized worldwide including Australia
varieties.traits: Low-growing creeper, clover-like leaves, yellow flowers that close at night
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific ground cover, continuous small leaf production
varieties.availability: Often naturalized; available from Nurserylive India (ships plants), or collect from naturalized populations in humid subtropical climates

varieties.name: Purple Wood Sorrel
varieties.origin: South America, ornamental selection
varieties.traits: Purple/maroon butterfly-shaped leaves, pale lilac flowers
varieties.availability: Garden centers throughout humid subtropical climates and Australia as ornamental houseplant/garden plant

varieties.name: Gongura Red Stem 🏆
varieties.origin: Traditional variety grown in South India for generations
varieties.traits: Red-stemmed variety, more sour than green type, grows 1-2m tall
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive in hot climates, continuous leaf harvest
varieties.availability: Local tropical monsoon climates markets, agricultural supply stores in Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, tropical monsoon climates; saved seeds from traditional growers

varieties.name: Gongura Green Stem 🏆
varieties.origin: Traditional South Indian variety
varieties.traits: Green stems and leaf stalks, milder than red variety
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates agricultural markets, local seed suppliers, saved seeds from traditional farmers

varieties.name: Puntikura 🏆
varieties.origin: Telangana traditional variety, grown across South India
varieties.traits: Regional selection of gongura, adapted to specific areas

varieties.name: Ambaadi 🏆
varieties.origin: Maharashtra traditional variety, grown across India
varieties.traits: Same species as gongura, different regional selection
varieties.availability: Maharashtra markets, pan-India seed suppliers, specialty stores

varieties.name: Indian Sorrel 🏆
varieties.origin: Traditional Indian variety, possibly native from India to Malaysia
varieties.traits: Tropical shrub 1.5-2.5m, pale yellow flowers, red calyces
varieties.availability: Local tropical monsoon climates nurseries, agricultural supply stores, home gardens

varieties.name: Mesta 🏆
varieties.origin: Indian subcontinent, especially Ganges Delta region
varieties.traits: Primarily grown for fiber but leaves also edible, 2-3m tall
varieties.availability: Agricultural supply stores, fiber crop suppliers, traditional farmers

varieties.name: Red Sorrel Traditional 🏆
varieties.origin: Traditional Indian variety of Hibiscus sabdariffa
varieties.traits: Shorter, bushier than fiber type, grown for food
varieties.yield_notes: Good calyx production for food use
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates home gardens, local markets, seed saved from traditional cultivation

varieties.name: Chichoda Bhaji 🏆
varieties.origin: Native to India, traditional edible weed
varieties.traits: Small creeping herb, trifoliate leaves, yellow flowers
varieties.availability: Naturally occurring in tropical monsoon climates gardens; available from Nurserylive India, Gachwala

varieties.name: Tin Patia 🏆
varieties.origin: Indian traditional name for Oxalis corniculata
varieties.traits: Same as Chichoda Bhaji, regional name variation
varieties.availability: Found growing naturally, available from Gachwala and Indian plant nurseries

varieties.name: Changeri 🏆
varieties.origin: Regional Indian name for wood sorrel

varieties.name: Purple Indian Wood Sorrel 🌱
varieties.origin: Variety of Oxalis corniculata with purple leaves
varieties.traits: Purple-red foliage, ornamental and edible
varieties.availability: Garden centers in tropical monsoon climates as ornamental, naturally occurring in some areas

varieties.name: French Sorrel (Buckler Leaf) 🏆
varieties.origin: Rumex scutatus, European origin
varieties.traits: Shield-shaped leaves, low-growing 15-45cm

varieties.name: Red-Veined Sorrel 🏆
varieties.origin: Rumex sanguineus, European
varieties.traits: Green leaves with red veins, ornamental and edible
varieties.availability: Specialty herb importers, international seed suppliers

varieties.name: Rosella Green Calyx 🌱
varieties.origin: Hibiscus sabdariffa selection with green calyces
varieties.traits: Yellow-green calyces, grown for leaves primarily
varieties.availability: Agricultural supply stores in India, specialty suppliers

varieties.name: Hibiscus Albus
varieties.origin: White/pale form of Hibiscus sabdariffa
varieties.traits: Pale calyces, similar growth to red types
varieties.availability: Specialty Hibiscus collectors, agricultural research stations

varieties.name: Hibiscus Ruber 🌱
varieties.origin: Deep red form of Hibiscus sabdariffa
varieties.traits: Intensely red calyces and stems

varieties.name: Wild Rosella 🏆
varieties.origin: Naturalized Hibiscus sabdariffa in tropical regions
varieties.traits: Feral populations adapted to local conditions
varieties.availability: Wild collection possible; seed from naturalized plants

varieties.name: Palak Ambadi
varieties.origin: Traditional tropical monsoon climates/South Indian leafy green
varieties.traits: Leafy green with sour flavor profile
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates local markets, home gardens, agricultural supply stores

varieties.name: Puli Keerai 🏆
varieties.origin: South Indian traditional sour leafy green
varieties.traits: Traditional sour green, may include multiple species
varieties.yield_notes: Traditional crop, reliable in tropical conditions

varieties.name: Sheep Sorrel (Wild Type) 🏆
varieties.origin: Rumex acetosella, European origin, naturalized in some Indian regions
varieties.traits: Small creeping plant, arrow-shaped leaves
varieties.availability: May be found naturalized at higher elevations; international seed suppliers

varieties.name: Profusion (Non-Flowering)
varieties.origin: USA cultivar, Rumex acetosa
varieties.traits: Non-flowering, compact, continuous production

varieties.name: Mountain Sorrel
varieties.origin: Alpine/mountain species, Oxyria digyna
varieties.traits: Cool-climate specialist, small leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Low to moderate in suitable climate
varieties.availability: Alpine plant specialists, rarely available in India
